summary: Manage commercial contract negotiation with term analysis, concession planning, and legal routing for non-standard terms.

inputs.name: contract_terms
inputs.description: Current or proposed contract terms and conditions.

inputs.name: negotiation_objectives
inputs.description: Desired outcomes, priorities, and walk-away points.

inputs.name: counterparty_position
inputs.description: Known counterparty demands, constraints, and leverage.

outputs.name: negotiation_strategy
outputs.description: Structured negotiation plan with tactics and sequencing.

outputs.name: concession_matrix
outputs.description: Planned concessions with trade-off options and limits.

outputs.name: legal_routing_plan
outputs.description: Non-standard terms requiring legal review with routing path.

budget.notes: Focus on protecting value while enabling deal progress.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is commercial negotiation, not legal contract drafting.
Require contract terms and negotiation objectives before planning.
Return strategy, concession matrix, and legal routing in one consistent output.

steps.action: Analyze contract terms against standard playbook and identify risks.
Design concession matrix with trade-off options and approval limits.
Identify non-standard terms requiring legal review and define routing.
Compile negotiation strategy with sequencing and fallback positions.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read contract drafts, term playbooks, and deal context.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Save negotiation strategy and concession matrix.

verification.method: Check that all contract terms are compared against standard playbook.
Check that concession limits and approval requirements are defined.
Check that non-standard terms have legal routing recommendations.

success_criteria: The negotiation strategy protects value while enabling deal progress.
Concession limits are defined with approval requirements.
Non-standard terms are routed to legal for review.

rollback.when: If the strategy proposes concessions beyond authority or misreads terms, roll back to contract data and rerun.
